title: Construction Management
description: Planning, organizing, scheduling and managing construction projects. Includes preconstruction planning, cost and quality control, materials procurement, subcontractor management, start-up and close-out.

title: Construction Management for Mechanical, Electrical and Plumbing (MEP) Systems
description: 3 Investigation into the integrated approach of managing and scheduling the installation of MEP systems, including the study and analysis of basic mechanical, electrical and plumbing components in construction and their relationships to the overall building.

title: Soils and Foundations
description: (2;3) Study of the properties of subsurface materials and the principles of subsurface construction. Topics include soil classification and testing, soil mechanics and foundation systems.

title: Senior Design II
description: Perform design and management analysis of an engineering or construction product or process. Oral and written documentation required. Projects to be supplied by local construction industry whenever possible.

title: Special Problems
description: Individualized instruction in theoretical or experimental problems. Written report required.
prerequisites: Consent of instructor and program coordinator. May be repeated for credit.

title: Cooperative Education Internship  1 hour  Supervised industrial internship requiring a minimum of
description: of work per experience.
prerequisites: Consent of department. May be repeated for credit up to a maximum of 3 semester credit hours.

title: Construction Cost Estimating
description: (2;3) Procedures, techniques and systems of construction cost estimating. Includes work classification, quantity detailing, specification interpretation and bid preparation.

title: Construction Graphics and Modeling
description: (1,4) Interpretation and creation of construction plans using hand drafting, CAD and BIM methods.

title: Soils and Foundations
description: (2;3) Study of the properties of subsurface materials and the principles of subsurface construction. Topics include soil classification and testing, soil mechanics and foundation systems.

title: Construction Scheduling
description: (2;3) Study of construction scheduling utilizing current techniques including Critical Path Method (CPM), the Precedence Method (PM), the Program Evaluation and Review Technique (PERT) and a probabilistic method.

title: Steel Structures
description: Principles, analysis and methodologies for conceptual and detailed design of steel structures. Emphasis on the role of mechanics in modern structural engineering design specifications with a focus on load and resistance factor design. Topics include behavior and design of hot-rolled and cold-formed steel, connections, members frames and advanced analysis techniques.

title: Special Problems
description: Individualized instruction in theoretical or experimental problems. Written report required.
prerequisites: Consent of instructor and program coordinator. May be repeated for credit.

title: Occupational Safety and Liability
description: Study of basic concepts of accident prevention, safety education, economic impact and environmental hazard control. Includes OSHA regulations and other regulations as they relate to the employer, the employee and the public.

title: Problems in Project Management
description: Construction project management simulation involving bid preparation, cost control, scheduling, contract preparation, construction documents interpretation, punchlist management and project evaluation.

title: Introduction to Risk Management in Construction
description: Introduction to risk management strategies by identifying potential risks and assigning mitigation control measures.

title: Steel Structures
description: Principles, analysis and methodologies for conceptual and detailed design of steel structures. Emphasis on the role of mechanics in modern structural engineering design specifications with a focus on load and resistance factor design. Topics include behavior and design of hot-rolled and cold-formed steel, connections, members frames and advanced analysis techniques.

title: Advanced Design in Cold-Formed Steel Structures
description: (2;3) Study of the theories of design and behavior of cold-formed/light gauge steel structural members, connections and systems. Relevant design specifications and computer applications are included.

title: Construction Methods and Materials
description: Introduction to the materials, systems, methods and procedures of building construction.

title: Surveying for Construction
description: (2;3) Surveying principles, instruments, measurements and calculations fundamentals of surveying for building construction; survey drawings and mapping.

title: Quality Management in Construction
description: Introduction to the principles and techniques associated with quality management and six sigma concepts as practiced in the construction industry.

title: Building Construction Techniques
description: (1,4) Contemporary techniques used in the construction industry; nature, use and characteristics of materials; construction methodology, application and sequencing in the building process. Course will include hands-on construction of a scale model of a building.

title: Senior Design II
description: (2;3) Perform design and management analysis of an engineering or construction product or process. Oral and written documentation required. Projects to be supplied by local construction industry whenever possible.
